summary: Russian forces attempted a cross-river infiltration across the frozen Siverskyi Donets, deploying MT-LB armored vehicles, motorcycles, and small infantry groups from occupied Sieversk, but were repelled by Ukrainian search and strike operations. Ukrainian FPV drones killed Russian propagandist and WarGonzo co-founder Evgeny Nikolayev ("Hayduk") in Donetsk region on March 10. Russian army aviation struck a Ukrainian UAV control point in Kolodizhne, Kharkiv region, while "Yolka" FPV air defense drones continued intercepting Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. Russian "Molniya" drones, now equipped with log-periodic antennas, are being used to target Ukrainian UAV control points and chase survivors with FPV drones in Sumy region. The "Rubikon" Center reported 11 enemy targets destroyed on March 16, including Ukrainian Gepard SPAAGs and UAV control points. Iranian-backed militias used fiber-optic FPV drones to attack U.S. Victory Base in Iraq, likely adopting Russian tactics. Ukrainian FPV pilots are disrupting Russian infantry movements, with footage showing a Russian soldier charging a drone with a bayonet and being struck by two simultaneous FPV hits.
